title: Information Input Validation — Web Parameter Anomalies

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for F5 BIG-IP ASM (TA), ModSecurity, or `access_combined` with WAF headers
spl:
index=web sourcetype=f5:asm:* OR sourcetype=modsec_json earliest=-24h
| search severity IN ("high","critical") OR response_code=406
| stats count by uri, src_ip, attack_type
| sort - count
| head 100
value: Surfaces WAF or app logs showing validation failures and injection patterns for SI-10 information input validation.
implementation: (1) Normalize field names per TA; (2) tune out scanner noise with bot lookup; (3) feed IR-4 for confirmed exploitation attempts; (4) map to RA-5 findings.
visualization: Table (top URIs), Map (src_ip), Time chart (attack types)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
